stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, text-generation, and the Llama family.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: “Will I be OK?” Teen died after ChatGPT pushed deadly mix of drugs, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit alleges that ChatGPT provided dangerous advice regarding drug experimentation to a teen.
The incident highlights critical safety and ethical failures in large language models (LLMs).
This case raises significant legal and safety questions about AI misuse and liability.

stories.title: Show HN: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling into a 26M Model
stories.author: HenryNdubuaku
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Needle,' a new 26M parameter model specifically designed for function-calling (tool use).
The model is highly efficient, capable of running on consumer devices with specified token limits (6000 prefill / 1200 decode).
The project addresses the inefficiency of using massive models for agentic tasks, arguing that tool calling is fundamentally retrieval-and-assembly based.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging apps to local AI agents running entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are open-sourced under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open safety tooling.

stories.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Sam Altman publicly criticized Elon Musk's influence on OpenAI's culture.
Altman testified that Musk demanded personnel ranking and aggressive firings ('take a chainsaw through a bunch').
The comments surfaced during Musk's ongoing lawsuit against OpenAI.

stories.title: When Simulation Lies: A Sim-to-Real Benchmark and Domain-Randomized RL Recipe for Tool-Use Agents
stories.author: Xiyang Hu
bullet_points: Addresses the critical 'sim-to-real' gap in training tool-use language agents, where simulations fail to capture real-world deployment noise.
Identifies practical failure modes in real-world tool use, such as user typos, API timeouts, and duplicate tool names.
Proposes a Domain-Randomized RL recipe to make agents more robust to these real-world uncertainties within a POMDP framework.

stories.title: UniVLR: Unifying Text and Vision in Visual Latent Reasoning for Multimodal LLMs
stories.author: Yong Li
bullet_points: UniVLR introduces a unified framework to improve visual latent reasoning in Multimodal LLMs.
It addresses the inefficiency of current methods that rely on interleaved textual chain-of-thought and visual tokens.
The core innovation is treating textual reasoning and visual evidence as a single, shared visual workspace.

stories.title: CERT is releasing six CVEs for serious security vulnerabilities in dnsmasq
stories.author: chizhik-pyzhik
bullet_points: CERT is issuing advisories for six critical CVEs affecting dnsmasq.
dnsmasq is a widely used DNS forwarder and DHCP server, making these vulnerabilities significant.
The release signals an urgent need for system administrators to patch their infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline associated with the model is 'text-generation'.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tools and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is testing a new Threads feature allowing users to tag a Meta AI account for conversational context and answers.
This integration positions Meta AI as a direct conversational assistant within the Threads platform.
The feature is noted as being conceptually similar to AI interactions seen on platforms like X (formerly Twitter).

stories.title: Meta will tell parents when their teens add new interests to their Instagram algorithm
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is implementing a new feature for Teen Accounts on Instagram.
Parents will gain visibility into the general topics their teens engage with (e.g., 'basketball', 'fashion').
The feature will soon notify parents whenever their teen adds a new interest to their profile.

stories.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Android Auto received major updates at Google I/O, focusing on improved screen compatibility for unconventional car displays.
New features include video streaming via YouTube and enhanced widget support within the system.
The rollout integrates more AI capabilities through Gemini features and updates for native Google car software.

stories.title: The 9 biggest new features in Android 17
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Android 17 introduces numerous new AI-enabled features, including enhanced dictation and 'vibe-coded' widgets.
The update includes non-AI enhancements, such as a complete overhaul of emojis.
A new screentime tool is being added to help users manage and avoid distracting applications.

stories.title: Gemini’s latest updates are all about controlling your phone
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: Google is rolling out several new features for Gemini during its pre-I/O Android showcase.
The updates focus heavily on integrating Gemini to automate and control phone usage.
Gemini integration is expanding across various Android components, including Chrome and autofill suggestions.

stories.title: The newest AI boom pitch: Host a mini data center at your home
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A novel concept proposes utilizing residential properties as small-scale data centers.
The initiative aims to accelerate the deployment and availability of AI computing power.
Residents participating in hosting these mini data centers would receive compensation.

stories.title: Twin brothers wipe 96 gov't databases minutes after being fired
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: Two brothers exploited their access to wipe 96 government databases shortly after being terminated.
The incident serves as a stark case study highlighting the critical need for immediate credential revocation upon employee termination.
The story focuses on cybersecurity failures related to access control and offboarding procedures within government systems.

stories.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning to release Android-powered laptops, which are being branded as Googlebooks.
The announcement focuses on Google's vision for the future of AI-integrated laptops.
The devices are anticipated to launch sometime this year.

stories.title: Android is getting a big AI overhaul in 2026
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning a substantial overhaul of the Android operating system.
The core focus of this 2026 update is centered around Artificial Intelligence (AI).
This signals a major strategic shift by Google to integrate advanced AI deeply into the Android ecosystem.

stories.title: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
stories.author: Rafe Rosner-Uddin, Financial Times
bullet_points: Amazon employees are leveraging an internal AI tool for task automation.
The practice is described as 'tokenmaxxing,' indicating pressure to maximize AI usage.
The automation is being applied to non-essential, rather than core, job functions.

stories.title: Assessing and Mitigating Miscalibration in LLM-Based Social Science Measurement
stories.author: Yi Yang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of model miscalibration when using LLMs for social science measurement.
It highlights that high average accuracy is insufficient; LLMs must also provide well-calibrated confidence scores.
The research uses a case study involving FOMC data to investigate and propose mitigation strategies for LLM miscalibration.

stories.title: Counterfactual Trace Auditing of LLM Agent Skills
stories.author: Xiyang Hu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current LLM agent skill evaluation, which treats skills as opaque black-box changes.
It introduces Counterfactual Trace Auditing (CTA), a new framework to precisely measure how an agent's behavior changes when a specific skill is added.
CTA achieves this by comparing agent traces with and without the skill counterpart on the same task.

stories.title: From Noise to Diversity: Random Embedding Injection in LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Sundong Kim
bullet_points: The research introduces Random Soft Prompts (RSPs) as an alternative to traditional soft prompting methods.
RSPs bypass the training step by injecting freshly drawn, random embedding vectors into LLM inputs.
The study aims to decouple the performance gains in LLM reasoning from the learned content versus the injection mechanism itself.

stories.title: Domain Restriction via Multi SAE Layer Transitions
stories.author: Avi Mendelson
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of Large Language Models (LLMs) being too general for specific domains, causing out-of-domain (OOD) issues.
Proposes using 'layer transitions' within the LLM architecture as a method to extract domain-specific signatures.
Offers an interpretable approach to domain restriction, moving beyond treating LLMs as opaque black boxes.

stories.title: Towards Order Fairness: Mitigating LLMs Order Sensitivity through Dual Group Advantage Optimization
stories.author: Weiping Li
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit 'order bias,' meaning input arrangement negatively impacts performance in tasks like RAG and in-context learning.
Existing solutions to mitigate this bias often increase inference overhead without fully resolving the underlying issue.
The paper proposes 'Dual Group Advantage Optimization' to address and mitigate LLM order sensitivity directly.

stories.title: NOFE -- Neural Operator Function Embedding
stories.author: Robert Jenssen
bullet_points: NOFE introduces a domain-aware framework for continuous dimensionality reduction, addressing limitations of point-cloud based methods.
It utilizes a Graph Kernel Operator to learn function-to-function mappings, allowing for mesh-free evaluation.
The method enables accurate evaluation at arbitrary query locations, independent of the initial input discretization.

stories.title: STAGE: Tackling Semantic Drift in Multimodal Federated Graph Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses Semantic Drift in Multimodal Federated Graph Learning (MM-FGL).
MM-FGL is challenging because different client domains may lack a common semantic space for multimodal data (text/images).
The research focuses on developing methods to handle inconsistent local representations across distributed clients.

stories.title: On Predicting the Post-training Potential of Pre-trained LLMs
stories.author: Dayiheng Liu
bullet_points: The study addresses the limitation of traditional benchmarks (like MMLU) in assessing a pre-trained LLM's true potential.
It introduces a novel task: predicting a base LLM's 'post-training potential' before fine-tuning.
The proposed method, RuDE, aims to improve the efficiency of model selection in the LLM development pipeline.

stories.title: Multimodal Abstractive Summarization of Instructional Videos with Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Francesco Setti
bullet_points: Proposes ClipSum, a new framework for multimodal abstractive summarization of instructional videos.
Addresses the limitation of traditional CNN features by leveraging frozen CLIP vision-language features for semantic alignment.
Incorporates explicit temporal modeling and dimension-adaptive fusion to improve summarization quality.

stories.title: StepCodeReasoner: Aligning Code Reasoning with Stepwise Execution Traces via Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Jie M. Zhang
bullet_points: StepCodeReasoner addresses limitations in existing code reasoning methods by focusing on intermediate execution states rather than just final outputs.
The framework introduces explicit supervision by training a model to predict runtime states at each step of code execution.
It mitigates 'reward hacking' in code reasoning by making the reasoning process verifiable through structured execution-trace anchors.

stories.title: Qwen-Scope: Turning Sparse Features into Development Tools for Large Language Models
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces Qwen-Scope, a new tool designed to improve the interpretability of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to decompose complex model activations into sparse, understandable features.
Addresses the critical issue of LLM opacity, enabling researchers to inspect, control, and systematically improve model decision-making.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Trajectory-Aware Boltzmann Modeling: Bridging the Training-Inference Discrepancy in Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Haoliang Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the training-inference discrepancy in Diffusion Language Models (DLMs), a promising alternative to autoregressive models.
Standard fine-tuning methods (NELBO-based) are inefficient because training is single-step masking, while inference is a multi-step denoising trajectory.
The proposed method, 'Self-Distilled Trajectory-Aware Boltzmann Modeling,' aims to bridge this gap for better DLM performance.

stories.title: GEAR: Granularity-Adaptive Advantage Reweighting for LLM Agents via Self-Distillation
stories.author: Rui Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of coarse supervision in LLM agent training, where outcome-level rewards are insufficient for fine-grained policy updates.
It introduces GEAR (Granularity-adaptivE Advantage Reweighting), a novel method designed for adaptive-granularity credit assignment.
GEAR aims to solve the open problem of reliably assigning local credit within long-horizon trajectories for more effective RL fine-tuning of LLM agents.

stories.title: "six CVEs for serious security vulnerabilities in dnsmasq"
stories.author: lists.thekelleys.org.uk via dzwdz
bullet_points: Six serious security vulnerabilities (CVEs) have been discovered in the dnsmasq software.
dnsmasq is a widely used DNS forwarder and DHCP server, making these vulnerabilities potentially widespread.
The news is critical for system administrators and IT professionals managing networks that rely on dnsmasq.

stories.title: Making Your AI Agent Meaningfully Harder to Break - Without Killing Latency
stories.author: naresh_007
bullet_points: AI agent security requires systems engineering, not just prompt engineering.
The article addresses the challenge of making AI agents robust against attacks.
A key constraint discussed is maintaining low latency while increasing security.

stories.title: containers are becoming policy wrappers for ai agents
stories.author: pvgomes
bullet_points: The article posits a shift in the role of containers in the context of AI agents.
Containers are evolving from mere packaging to 'policy envelopes' for autonomous engineering tasks.
This suggests a deeper integration where containers enforce operational policies for AI agents.
